perturbation.original_text: XSPA shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to display, market, promote, offer for sale and sell the Products set forth on Exhibit A in all Stores throughout the Territory. The Products shall only be sold by XSPA at the retail price of the applicable Product as set forth on Exhibit A (the "Retail Price"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Retail Price for the Products at the Stores in the Territory (i) may only be modified by mutual agreement of the Parties, and (ii) does not include any discounts, promotions or applicable sales taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: XSPA shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to display, market, promote, offer for sale and sell the Products set forth on Exhibit A in all Stores throughout the Territory. The Products shall be sold by XSPA at a price determined solely by XSPA's discretion, which may or may not align with the retail price of the applicable Product as set forth on Exhibit A (the "Retail Price"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Retail Price for the Products at the Stores in the Territory (i) may be modified unilaterally by XSPA, and (ii) includes discounts, promotions and applicable sales taxes as determined by XSPA.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2.01 states that 'The Products shall only be sold by XSPA at the retail price of the applicable Product as set forth on Exhibit A,' and that this price 'may only be modified by mutual agreement of the Parties.' The changed text allows XSPA to set prices at their discretion and modify the prices unilaterally. The contradiction arises because later sections of the agreement assume that the Retail Price, as originally defined, is binding, especially Section 8.01 which calculates payments based on a percentage of the Retail Price.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XSPA shall pay to Calm on a monthly basis an amount equal to (i) fifty percent (50%) of the Retail Price for all Products sold in the Stores in the Territory during the applicable month minus (ii) fifty percent (50%) of any commission actually paid or payable to XSPA employee(s) or contractor(s) attributable to sales of such Products during such month; provided that in no event shall such commission be greater than fifteen percent (15%) of the Retail Price for the applicable Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.01 fundamentally alters the pricing mechanism, allowing XSPA to set and modify prices unilaterally, which directly contradicts Section 8.01. Section 8.01 calculates payments to Calm based on a percentage of the 'Retail Price', which the original Section 2.01 defined as fixed and modifiable only by mutual agreement. The modification creates an ambiguity in how the 'Retail Price' is defined and used for payment calculation, leading to a direct contradiction and uncertainty about the financial obligations under the contract. This represents a strong, obvious contradiction, meeting the criteria for 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Calm or its authorized designees shall be the sole suppliers to XSPA of XSPA's requirements for Products, Gifts, Shopping Bags, Uniforms and Inserts (collectively, "Product Collateral") for each Store throughout the Territory. Calm shall use reasonable efforts to fulfill all of XSPA's requirements for Product Collateral in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event that inventory of any Product Collateral in any Store falls below fifty percent (50%) of the initial amount of such Product Collateral provided to such Store, XSPA shall use best efforts to advise Calm in sufficient detail to enable Calm to ship additional inventory of such Product Collateral to such Store.
perturbation.changed_text: Calm or its authorized designees may be one of multiple suppliers to XSPA of XSPA's requirements for Products, Gifts, Shopping Bags, Uniforms and Inserts (collectively, "Product Collateral") for each Store throughout the Territory. While Calm will attempt to fulfill XSPA's needs, XSPA is free to seek Product Collateral from other sources. In the event that inventory of any Product Collateral in any Store falls below fifty percent (50%) of the initial amount of such Product Collateral provided to such Store, XSPA is not obligated to inform Calm and may source additional inventory of such Product Collateral from other suppliers.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 5.05 designates Calm as the 'sole supplier' of Product Collateral. The modified version contradicts this by stating Calm may be one of multiple suppliers. This creates a contradiction with the provision that states 'Calm shall retain legal title to any and all Product Collateral', which now becomes unclear if XSPA is sourcing from other providers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that, as between the Parties, Calm shall retain legal title to any and all Product Collateral (other than Uniforms) until sold in a Store in the Territory or otherwise disbursed in a Store in the Territory (in the case of Gifts, Inserts and Shopping Bags) in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event Calm identifies any issue with any Product Collateral making it unsuitable for use as contemplated by this Agreement or issues a recall with respect to any Product, XSPA shall promptly return any and all affected Product Collateral to Calm or its authorized designees upon receiving notice of such issue or recall.
perturbation.justification: The original text designates Calm as the sole supplier, which directly conflicts with the modified text stating Calm may be one of multiple suppliers. This fundamentally alters the agreement, especially concerning 'Calm shall retain legal title to any and all Product Collateral'. The modified section removes the sole supplier obligation and allows XSPA to source from other suppliers and not obligated to inform Calm, thus it directly contradicts the clause about Calm retaining title, as XSPA may now have collateral Calm does not have title to, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any action contemplated by this Section ​9.06 shall be controlled by the Party with ownership of the relevant Marks, or in the case of Products, Calm.
perturbation.changed_text: Any action contemplated by this Section ​9.06 shall be controlled solely by Calm, regardless of who owns the relevant Marks or Products.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9.06 originally stated that action related to intellectual property infringement would be controlled by the owner of the relevant marks, but in the case of products, Calm. The modified text gives Calm sole control over all actions, regardless of mark ownership. This contradicts other parts of Section 9 which explicitly grants rights to XSPA concerning XSPA's marks. The contradiction primarily affects 9.04, which says 'Neither Party shall use the other Party's Marks...'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Party shall use the other Party's Marks, in whole or in part, as a corporate name, trade name or domain name and shall not use the other Party's Marks in combination with any other mark, design or designation except pursuant to the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall use the other Party's Marks in the Territory strictly in compliance with all applicable legal requirements of the Territory. Each Party acknowledges and agrees that, as between the Parties, the other Party is the sole and exclusive owner of the Marks licensed by such other Party pursuant to this Section ​9 and all goodwill associated therewith. Neither Party shall do or cause to be done any act or thing that may in any way adversely affect any rights of the other Party in and to such other Party's Marks or any registrations thereof or that, directly or indirectly, may reduce the value of such Marks or detract from any Mark's reputation, including challenging the ownership, validity or enforceability of such Marks. Each Party agrees that it will display the trademark registration symbol ® or the designations "SM" or "TM" adjacent to the Marks when directed to do so by the Party owning the relevant Mark and, when circumstances reasonably permit, it will cause a notice of ownership to appear on advertisements or in store displays bearing the Marks. Each Party will take care to display the other Party's Marks in a manner that does not bring the other Party's brand into disrepute.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Calm solely controls actions related to Section 9.06, regardless of ownership of relevant Marks or Products. This directly contradicts the original agreement in Section 9.04, which explicitly grants each party rights regarding their respective Marks, stating, "Neither Party shall use the other Party's Marks..." The modification effectively nullifies the rights XSPA has concerning its own marks as described in Section 9.04, because the modified text gives sole control to Calm in 9.06. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.
